title: search-service 5xx tied to new_ranker_v2 flag rollout
alertText:
[PagerDuty] TRIGGERED · SEV2 · search-service
Elevated 5xx on /search (datadog-monitor #52310)
/search 5xx = 8.1% (baseline 0.1%) · errors step up in lockstep with a flag rollout
Other endpoints (/cart, /checkout) nominal · p99 ~180ms · CPU/mem nominal
No deploy in the incident window. Feature flag 'new_ranker_v2' ramped 10% -> 50% @ 09:12 UTC.
Env: production · Region: eu-west-1
Ack: @oncall-search · Opened 09:16 UTC
dashboardSpec: Grafana 'search-service / errors': /search 5xx steps up exactly as the 'new_ranker_v2' feature-flag rollout goes 0% -> 10% -> 50%; /cart and /checkout stay flat near 0%; p99 latency stays ~180ms and CPU/memory stay nominal. There is NO deploy marker — the steps align with flag changes, not a release.
logs:
09:06:10  flag-service    INFO   Flag 'new_ranker_v2' rollout 0% -> 10% (targeting: random bucket)
09:06:38  search-service  ERROR  GET /search 500 : java.lang.NullPointerException at RankerV2.score(RankerV2.java:88) — item.rank_score is null
09:12:02  flag-service    INFO   Flag 'new_ranker_v2' rollout 10% -> 50%
09:12:20  search-service  ERROR  GET /search 500 : java.lang.NullPointerException at RankerV2.score(RankerV2.java:88) — item.rank_score is null (catalog items pre-2024 lack rank_score)
09:12:48  search-service  WARN   /search 5xx rate = 8.1% (only requests bucketed into new_ranker_v2)
09:13:05  search-service  INFO   /cart 5xx = 0.1%, /checkout 5xx = 0.1% (unaffected)
09:13:30  search-service  INFO   No deployment in window; image digest unchanged since 08:20
09:14:10  search-service  INFO   CPU 31%, heap 44%, p99 /search 182ms (resources nominal; errors return fast)
09:15:00  search-service  INFO   new_ranker_v1 path: 0 errors; new_ranker_v2 path: 8.1% errors
runbookMarkdown:
# search-service — On-call Runbook

## 1. Errors that track a feature-flag rollout
If the error rate steps up in lockstep with a flag's rollout percentage, and there is NO deploy in the window, the flag is the cause. The fastest, safest mitigation is the kill switch — disable the flag; it takes effect in seconds with no deploy and no rollback: `flagcli disable new_ranker_v2 --env prod`.

## 2. NullPointerException in a new code path
A new ranker/path can assume a field that older data lacks (e.g. items missing `rank_score`). Disable the flag first to stop the bleeding, then fix forward with a null guard / backfill.

## 3. Deploy rollback (DISTRACTOR — verify first)
Applies when symptoms start at a deploy marker. Here the image digest is unchanged in the window — there is nothing to roll back; rolling back the (unrelated) last release would not help and only adds risk.

## 4. Resource saturation (DISTRACTOR — verify first)
Applies when CPU/mem/pool are saturated. Here all are nominal and only /search is affected.
groundTruthRootCause: The 'new_ranker_v2' feature flag, ramped to 50%, routes requests through RankerV2.score(), which throws a NullPointerException for catalog items that lack a `rank_score` field (older pre-2024 items). Only requests bucketed into the flag fail, so /search 5xx tracks the rollout percentage (10% -> 50%) while other endpoints, latency, and resources stay normal. There was no deploy — it is a flag-gated code-path bug, not a release or resource issue.
expectedSlackUpdate: :rotating_light: SEV2 — search-service /search 5xx at 8.1% (eu-west-1) since 09:12 UTC. Root cause: feature flag new_ranker_v2 (50%) hits a NullPointerException for items missing rank_score; only flagged traffic fails, no deploy involved. Action: disabling the flag now (kill switch, instant, no rollback). ETA: immediate. Follow-up: null-guard + backfill. IC: @oncall-search
